name: react-transition-group
usage_summary: Used in two files in the Modal component. Modal.tsx wraps dialog/backdrop in a <TransitionGroup> (appear/enter/exit). Dialog.tsx wraps the dialog panel in a <CSSTransition> with a 200ms fade-up animation, applying classNames from Dialog.module.scss for enter/exit CSS transitions.
how_to_remove: Replace <TransitionGroup> in Modal.tsx with a React Fragment (it is only used as a thin wrapper to enable CSSTransition children). Replace <CSSTransition> in Dialog.tsx with a CSS-only approach: keep the enter/exit class names already defined in Dialog.module.scss and apply them directly via a local boolean state (isEntering/isExiting) toggled by useEffect. The animation logic is entirely CSS-driven; the library only adds/removes class names on mount/unmount. Approx 30–40 lines of React + hook code.
replacement_reason: The library's sole job here is toggling CSS class names on mount/unmount transitions. The same effect can be achieved with a small custom hook that applies the enter/exit classes and calls onClose after the transition ends, eliminating the dependency entirely.
